Patch Set 5:

Hi, JinShan, We still need consider the distro without truncate_inode_pages_final defined. Like the RHEL6 and SLES. Maybe move this part to lustre_compat25.h is properly. I don't know why you change mind to give + for this patch. I think you are right in previous comment. The second sync_filesystem not reached ll_writepages. I am still investigating the reason.

Patch Set 5:

You're right. We need to implement our own truncate_inode_pages_final() if it doesn't exist, and fold our workaround into that function.

The inode may be put into the wb dirty list again with a new timestamp, therefore it was skipped in the 2nd __sync_filesystem() call. If you would like to know the exact reason, you can turn on write back tracing and probably add more track point yourself to understand what exactly is going on.
Uploaded patch set 6.

parents.subject: Revert "LU-5568 lnet: fix kernel crash when network failed to start"

commit.subject: LU-5584 llite: ensure all data flush out when umount
commit.message:
LU-5584 llite: ensure all data flush out when umount

Write out all extents when clear inode. Otherwise we
may lose data while umount.

Change-Id: I199217c96a7ba49c2f3577a65750e7956a9af63a
Signed-off-by: Yang Sheng <yang.sheng@intel.com>
Signed-off-by: Jinshan Xiong <jinshan.xiong@intel.com>
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/12103
Tested-by: Jenkins
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Bobi Jam <bobijam@gmail.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
